title: How to get a police certificate – New Zealand - Canada.ca
content:
## How to get a police certificate – New Zealand
If you need to give your fingerprints for a police certificate, this isn’t the same as giving your biometrics (fingerprints and photo) for an application.

## Name of the document you need
Request your own Criminal Conviction History

## Police certificate and IRCC requirements
You must provide a police certificate when you apply for permanent residence in Canada.
You will also be requested to provide a police certificate to Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) if you declared a criminal record on your application for an electronic travel authorization, work permit or study permit.
You must submit a criminal record check from the New Zealand Ministry of Justice. Request your own Criminal Conviction History with a full record of convictions.

## Special considerations
When completing the Request your own Criminal Conviction History form, you must request a full record of convictions.
The completed application form and a copy of your identification (ID) may be submitted to the Ministry of Justice by mail or email.
Email: criminalrecord@justice.govt.nz
In New Zealand Phone: 04 918 8800 and ask to speak to a Criminal Records Officer
Overseas Phone: +64 4 918 8800 and ask to speak to a Criminal Records Officer
Send us mail Postal address: Criminal Records Unit Ministry of Justice SX10161 Wellington
